# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud monitoring and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

# gcp-appstack/main.tf
resource "google_compute_instance" "default" {
  name         = var.gce_name ?? "my-server" # default to 'my-server' if not provided in the variable block or passed as an argument when calling this resource directly, e.g., gcp_appstack::main(null) -> google_compute_instance
  machine_type = var.machine_type ?? "n1-standard" # default to 'n1-standard' if not provided in the variable block or passed as an argument when calling this resource directly, e.g., gcp_appstack::main(null) -> google
}
